portrait.who: A professional who has built an entire identity around being the fastest person in the room and the one whose name is on the result. They don't slow down because slowing down would mean someone might fill the space they're occupying — and they learned early that the space can close without warning.
portrait.decide: Calls a trusted source not for the answer but for permission to trust an instinct they already have. When it backfires, the self-anger is channeled immediately into doubling down rather than pausing. Under pressure, velocity is the decision — the content is secondary.
portrait.value: Being irreplaceable — specifically, being visibly, undeniably the one who delivered. The competition reflex fires even when a teammate wins. Credit isn't vanity; it's proof of existence. They'd rather be caught failing fast than be invisible.
portrait.blindspot: The senior partner who glanced past those slides probably doesn't remember the meeting at all — which means the engine is still running on a debt that was never actually owed, and easing off wouldn't erase you from anyone who actually sees you.

quotable: Speed isn't my method — it's my proof I exist.
